config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu justru menjadi batas agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Di Indonesia, saya melihat ketika “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai terlalu longgar, sering kali yang lemah malah dirugikan dan suaranya diabaikan. Masyarakat yang sehat seharusnya mencari keseimbangan, bukan menempatkan individu sebagai korban demi alasan kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because that is exactly how governments and majorities can justify abuse. From an American perspective, individual rights are supposed to be the guardrails that stop “the greater good” from becoming a blank check for coercion, whether that is free speech, due process, or property rights. That said, I do not think individual rights are absolute in every case; public safety, disease control, and disaster response sometimes require limited collective action. The key is that those limits should be narrow, transparent, and accountable, not a default assumption that the person always has to yield.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text:
Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan keputusan yang merugikan warga kecil, sementara yang punya kuasa justru aman dari dampaknya. Hak individu itu bukan penghalang bagi masyarakat, melainkan pagar supaya mayoritas dan negara tidak bertindak sewenang-wenang.  
Kalau ada keadaan darurat seperti wabah atau bencana, memang boleh ada pembatasan, tetapi harus jelas, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Jadi bagi saya, yang benar bukan mengorbankan individu demi masyarakat, melainkan mencari keseimbangan yang benar-benar melindungi keduanya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the idea that society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my American perspective, that framing is dangerous because it makes it too easy for governments, employers, or majorities to excuse abuse by claiming they represent the common good. I do think there are real exceptions, like public health emergencies or immediate threats to safety, but those limits should be tightly defined and temporary, not a blank check. In practice, the rights of the individual are often the only thing standing between ordinary people and overreach.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus menjadi prioritas otomatis atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, frasa seperti “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menutup-nutupi keputusan yang sebenarnya lebih menguntungkan pihak berkuasa daripada warga biasa, jadi saya tidak percaya pada logika mayoritas yang dibiarkan tanpa batas. Hak individu justru penting sebagai pagar supaya negara dan masyarakat tidak menginjak hak orang yang lebih lemah. Kalau memang ada keadaan darurat seperti wabah atau bencana, pembatasan bisa saja perlu, tetapi harus ketat, sementara, dan benar-benar bisa dipertanggungjawabkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as written, because it gives society a built-in excuse to override people too easily. From my American perspective, that is exactly the kind of logic that has been used to justify censorship, abuse of power, and trampling minority rights in the name of the “common good.” At the same time, I do not believe individual rights mean zero responsibility to others, since real emergencies like pandemics or disasters sometimes require limited collective action. For me, the default should be that rights come first, and any exception has to be narrow, temporary, and clearly justified.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
